history.content:
Hey Raul — welcome! I’m Prometheus, your dedicated assistant for the **Prometheus Growth** project, and I’m excited to build this with you.

Want to give me the quick story of the project—what it is, what outcomes you want, who’s involved (people/clients), what tools you’re using, and any deadlines you’re aiming for?  
We can keep it simple and shape it together from there.
Hey prom - this project is going to be talking about and working on getting it out there. I want to promote prometheus as an actual product and have people using it on a monthly subscription.

Perfect — got it. I’ve locked in that this project is about **marketing Prometheus as a real product** and **growing monthly subscribers**.

Next, give me quick answers to these 5 so I can build your go-to-market plan:
1) Who is your ideal first customer?
2) What core problem does Prometheus solve for them?
3) What monthly price are you considering?
4) Where do you want to promote first (X, LinkedIn, YouTube, communities, outbound, etc.)?
5) What’s your target for month 1 (e.g., 10 paying users)?

1) Anyone really - anyone looking to integrate into AI - 2) its a full CIS system - imagine connecting everything about your busness, and it knows eveyrthing and can act upon that - on top of being an all around general AI + teams, scheduled tasks, etc a bunch of stuff 3) lets figure that out 4) X and reddit 5) 10 would be great.

Love it — this is a strong direction.

Given your answers, here’s the move: **keep broad brand messaging**, but run growth with a **focused first wedge** so you can hit the 10 paid target faster.  
I recommend first wedge: **small business operators/founders who want “AI that actually runs operations,” not just chat.**

Next step I suggest (and can do right now): I’ll draft  
1) a simple **pricing model** (3 tiers),  
2) your **X + Reddit positioning statement**, and  
3) a **14-day launch sprint** aimed at first 10 subscribers.

Thats a great idea - im really thinking i want to get a whole team for this though - i need the full stack. I need a website created, i need marketing, etc. everything. SO lets plan this out dont create anything yet

100% — that’s exactly the right move.  
Let’s design the **full-stack growth team** first, then execute.

## Proposed Team Blueprint (Planning Only)

### 1) Leadership / Orchestration
- **Growth Lead (you + me)**
  - Owns priorities, offer, pricing decisions, weekly targets
- **Ops Coordinator**
  - Turns strategy into weekly sprint tasks, keeps everyone aligned

### 2) Product Marketing Core
- **Positioning & Messaging**
  - Clear “what Prometheus is / who it’s for / why now”
- **Offer & Pricing**
  - Define tiers, trial/guarantee, conversion path

### 3) Website & Conversion
- **Web Builder**
  - Landing page + core pages (Product, Pricing, Use Cases)
- **Conversion/CRO**
  - CTA flow, waitlist/signup funnel, analytics events, A/B tests

### 4) Demand Generation
- **X Growth Operator**
  - Daily content + engagement engine
- **Reddit Growth Operator**
  - Subreddit strategy, high-value posts/comments, credibility-first approach
- **Content Repurposer**
  - Turn one core idea into multiple channel assets

### 5) Sales / Customer Pipeline
- **Inbound Handler**
  - DMs, replies, follow-ups, demo scheduling
- **Onboarding + Retention**
  - First-value checklist, activation, testimonials/case studies

### 6) Data / Feedback Loop
- **Growth Analyst**
  - Weekly metrics: traffic → signup → paid conversion
- **Voice-of-Customer Collector**
  - Track objections, requests, language users actually use

## 30-Day Rollout Plan (High Level)

- **Week 1:** Positioning, ICP wedge, pricing draft, site structure/wireframe
- **Week 2:** Website MVP live + X/Reddit content system live
- **Week 3:** Push acquisition + tighten onboarding + collect first testimonials
- **Week 4:** Optimize conversion points + focus on closing first 10 paid users

## Operating Rhythm
- **Daily:** content + engagement + pipeline follow-up
- **2x weekly:** metrics + messaging review
- **Weekly:** strategic review + next sprint planning

If you want, next I’ll give you a **concrete org chart** with:
1) exact role responsibilities,  
2) KPIs per role, and  
3) the first **14-day execution board** (task-by-task) — still planning only, no creation yet.

yes thats beautiful - lets talk abt this all - i want an agent on the team to do a full deep analysis of prometheus and all of its features - and then keep that in a md file in the teams workspace, this way both the content posters and the website creator agent, and product marketer agetn  can add and say the right stuff - im thinking i want to have these files on github, and then deploy on vercel - so lets make sure the website builder knows that and creates the pages accordingly, no html  -

Perfect. This is exactly the right architecture decision.

## Locked Requirements (from you)
- Planning only right now (no asset creation yet)
- Dedicated **deep analysis agent** to document Prometheus/features
- Shared markdown source-of-truth used by:
  - website builder
  - product marketer
  - content posters
- Website workflow must be:
  - **GitHub-first**
  - **Vercel deployment**
  - **no raw standalone HTML** (framework pages/components only)

## Team Design (Full-Stack, Planning Mode)

### 1) **Product Intelligence Agent** (new critical role)
**Purpose:** Produce and maintain canonical product knowledge.

**Outputs (team workspace):**
- `[REDACTED-HE].md` (primary truth file)
- `[REDACTED-HE].md`
- `[REDACTED-HE].md`
- `[REDACTED-HE].md`

**What goes inside PRODUCT_BIBLE.md**
- What Prometheus is (CIS layer + general AI + automation)
- Core capabilities and constraints
- Who it is for / not for
- Value props by persona
- Proof points, examples, and objections/answers
- “Say/Don’t Say” claims policy

This agent should be the **upstream dependency** for marketing + website copy.

### 2) **Product Marketing Agent**
Uses PRODUCT_BIBLE to create:
- positioning narrative
- offer framing
- pricing hypotheses
- channel-specific message pillars

No freeform claims outside the source docs.

### 3) **Website Builder Agent** (GitHub + Vercel constrained)
**Hard constraints for this role:**
- Build in framework structure (Next.js preferred)
- No static raw-html page dumps
- Repo-backed workflow (branch/commit/PR style)
- Vercel-compatible project structure from day 1

**Expected page plan (later execution):**
- `/` Home
- `/product`
- `/use-cases`
- `/pricing`
- `/faq`
- `/contact` or `/book-demo`

All copy must reference PRODUCT_BIBLE / guardrails.

### 4) **Content Agent — X**
Creates posts/threads from source docs only:
- founder voice + authority + operational AI angle
- CTA toward website waitlist/demo
- no speculative claims

### 5) **Content Agent — Reddit**
- subreddit-fit educational posts/comments
- non-spam, proof-led, use-case-first writing
- strict compliance with community rules

### 6) **Growth Ops / Analytics Agent**
- defines funnel metrics
- tracks traffic → signup → activation → paid
- weekly learnings file in workspace

## Dependency Flow (important)
1. Product Intelligence builds core docs  
2. Product Marketing + Website + Content consume docs  
3. Growth Ops measures performance  
4. Feedback loops back into Product Intelligence docs weekly

## Repository + Deployment Blueprint (planning)
- GitHub repo: `prometheus-growth-site`
- Branch model: `main` + short-lived feature branches
- Vercel connected to repo
- Preview deploys per PR
- Production deploy from `main`

Framework recommendation: **Next.js + TypeScript** (cleanest Vercel path, no raw HTML workflow)

## Suggested Team Order (when we execute)
1. Stand up Product Intelligence agent first  
2. Have it generate initial PRODUCT_BIBLE set  
3. Lock messaging guardrails  
4. Then activate Website + Product Marketing + Content agents  
5. Finally activate Analytics agent for optimization loop

If you want, next I’ll draft the **exact agent roster** (IDs, responsibilities, tool permissions, and output file paths) so we can instantiate the team cleanly in one pass.

Thats fantastic - i love it, - also as for the X posting, im already logged into X on my computer -  so it can go ahead and post whenever. As for reddit we can forget reddit for now - but for X  it should make the posts before hand as well as laso - i think we need an engagement

Amazing — locked in.  
I’ve updated the project context with:

- **X as primary active channel**
- **Reddit paused for now**
- New role requirement for **X Engagement Agent**
- New role requirement for **X Content Queue Agent** (prewritten post bank)
- Note that you’re **already logged into X locally**, so direct posting automation is ready when we move from planning to execution

For the team plan, I recommend this X pod structure:
1) **X Strategist** (themes, cadence, hooks)  
2) **X Queue Writer** (writes/schedules posts ahead)  
3) **X Engagement Operator** (replies, conversations, trust-building)  
4) **X Performance Analyst** (tracks what converts to signups/paid)

If you want, next I’ll map this into a **weekly operating system** (exact daily responsibilities + KPIs per role), still planning-only.
Yup lets go ahead and do it - all one big team. Lets get this going

Done — it’s live. I created **one full-stack managed team** and wired it exactly to your spec.

### Team created
- **Name:** Prometheus Growth Full-Stack Team  
- **Team ID:** `team_mn74d1g9_0f4d45`
- **Kickoff review:** scheduled automatically (30s)

### Agents included
- `pg_product_intel_v1` (deep feature/capability dossier owner)
- `pg_product_marketer_v1`
- `pg_website_builder_v1` (**GitHub + Vercel + framework-only, no raw HTML deliverables**)
- `pg_x_queue_writer_v1` (prewritten post queue)
- `pg_x_engagement_operator_v1` (active engagement on X)
- `pg_growth_analyst_v1`
- `pg_ops_coordinator_v1`

I also updated your project `CONTEXT.md` with the new decision: **one big team, X-first with queue + engagement, Reddit paused**.

If you want, next I can immediately dispatch the first coordinated run:
1) product intel builds canonical docs,
2) marketer derives messaging,
3) website builder drafts framework page map,
4) X queue + engagement playbook starts.
also to make sure the path for the new team is set right! - "D:\Prometheus\workspace\teams\team_mn74d1g9_0f4d45"

Perfect — confirmed and saved.

I updated `[REDACTED-HE].md` with:

`D:\Prometheus\workspace\teams\team_mn74d1g9_0f4d45`

So the team workspace path is now explicitly locked in project context.

And why was this the first run?? "Manager
2m ago
Dispatching PG Ops Coordinator: Audit the latest completed scheduled run for this growth team and return a concise operations status pack with: (1) which subagent ran, (2) intended goal for that run, (3) whether the goal was actuall"

Great catch — you’re right to question it.

That happened because the manager’s **first auto-review** chose a **low-risk bootstrap action**: dispatch Ops Coordinator to audit the latest run state before starting production work.  
In short: it did a “state check” first instead of “build first,” which is safe but not what you wanted as the first visible action.

So yes — your expectation is correct: first run should have been **forward execution** (Product Intel dossier kickoff), not audit-only.

If you want, I’ll correct the flow immediately by forcing this order on the next dispatches:
1) `pg_product_intel_v1` → create canonical product dossier files  
2) `pg_product_marketer_v1` + `pg_website_builder_v1` consume that dossier  
3) `pg_x_queue_writer_v1` + `pg_x_engagement_operator_v1` start X system from approved messaging
